other_observers: Eric DeFonso (finder), Thomas Heinrich (photo taker) and mob.
specific_location: Lagerman Reservoir and the Swede Lakes
description: Medium sized shorebirds with upturned bills, but definitely smaller than the Marbled Godwits seen here earlier in April. Much darker plumage than Marbled Godwit, with deep red breasts and bellies and dark brown backs. The inner half of the bills were reddish.  I saw them fly twice when disturbed by a Bald Eagle, and the second time they came within 100 m when Thomas H took the attached photo. In flight they clearly showed the bright white patch in the upper tail, the thinnish white wingbar and the very dark underwing.
